stories.headline: FOMC verdict lands Wednesday with BTC pinned below $78K, futures market eyes 25bp cut
stories.tldr: The Fed wraps its two-day meeting Wednesday with crypto traders de-risked and BTC stalling under $78,000 ahead of the rate call.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A dovish surprise could ignite the bid that BTC has failed to find at $80K all month; a hawkish tone risks unwinding April's 13% rebound.
the_details: BTC has rejected $80,000 four times this month and slipped to ~$77,500 as the Coinbase premium turned negative for the first time since April 8.
BTC's 30-day correlation with the U.S. Dollar Index sits at -0.90, the most negative reading since 2022, making the Fed call the dominant variable.
Bitcoin futures open interest dropped over 6% in 24 hours into the meeting as traders unwound leverage and waited on Powell.

stories.headline: Atkins kills 'regulation by enforcement,' teases SEC innovation exemption for tokenized securities
stories.tldr: SEC Chair Paul Atkins formally ended the agency's enforcement-first crypto stance and said an innovation exemption for onchain tokenized securities is weeks away.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A workable carve-out for onchain securities would unlock RWA issuance from regulated US issuers and finally give DeFi venues a legal lane to host them.
the_details: Atkins announced the pivot on April 27, pairing it with planned joint guidance from the SEC and CFTC under their March 17 MOU.
On April 13, SEC's Division of Trading and Markets said it won't object to qualifying user-interface providers operating without broker-dealer registration.
Coalition of 100+ crypto firms is still pressing the Senate Banking Committee to mark up the CLARITY Act before a Memorial Day drop-dead window.

stories.headline: April becomes worst crypto-hack month in over a year as losses cross $606M across 12 incidents
stories.tldr: Twelve exploits have drained $606M from crypto in April, eclipsing all of Q1's losses combined and capping a contagion wave that started with Drift on April 1.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Cross-chain bridge risk and supply-chain attacks on web infra are now the dominant DeFi tail risk — TVL flight and insurance repricing follow.
the_details: Drift Protocol on Solana lost ~$285M on April 1 in an attack authorities tie to North Korea-linked social engineering.
Kelp DAO's LayerZero bridge was drained for ~$292M on April 19, minting 116,500 unbacked rsETH across 20 chains.
CoW Swap lost $1.2M to an April 14 domain hijack, and a Vercel breach forced crypto teams to rotate API keys days later.

stories.headline: Polygon hard fork triggers Upbit POL halt at 11:00 UTC April 29, sPOL launch teed up next
stories.tldr: Upbit will pause POL deposits and withdrawals at 11:00 UTC on April 29 ahead of a Polygon hard fork that paves the way for the sPOL liquid staking unlock.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: sPOL is Polygon's bid to recapture institutional staking flows lost to ETH and SOL, and the fork is the gating event for that liquidity narrative.
the_details: Upbit's pause covers POL and GMT and is described as standard precaution for the upcoming Polygon network upgrade.
The fork advances the MATIC-to-POL migration and lifts scalability and security parameters across the PoS chain.
Polygon team frames sPOL as the major DeFi liquidity unlock and a hook for institutional staking allocators.

stories.headline: Bittensor TAO rips +47% YTD as Q1 AI-services revenue prints $43M, market cap nears $3.5B
stories.tldr: Bittensor's TAO is up roughly 47% year-to-date with a market cap near $3.5B, after the network booked $43M in Q1 AI-services revenue.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: TAO is the cleanest 'crypto AI' revenue story right now and is decoupling from BTC's chop, pulling capital toward AI-native L1s ahead of summer.
the_details: TAO sits among ETH, XRP, SOL, and Hyperliquid as altcoins building independent momentum while BTC corrects ~20% from ATH.
Q1 2026 revenue of $43M comes from AI services running on Bittensor's subnet architecture.
Drivers cited include institutional flows, regulatory clarity from the SEC-CFTC MOU, and protocol-level upgrades.
